---
format: report-typst
title: "Project Title Goes Here"
subtitle: "Planning Grant Final Report"
report-author: "Jane Doe, Ph.D."
author-email: "jane.doe@university.edu"
author-affiliation: "Director, Example Institute"
institution: "Your University"
date: "2026-04-21"
# logo: "assets/logo.png"         # optional — path to your institution/project logo
# accent-color: "cc092f"          # hex without the # — defaults to a deep red
running-title: "Project Final Report"
running-footer: "Example Institute Planning Grant | Your University"
---

# Executive Summary

One or two paragraphs summarizing the scope of the report and the key
conclusions. Readers who only read this section should still come away
with an accurate picture of what the project accomplished, what was
learned, and what should happen next.

```{=typst}
#callout(type: "important", title: "Bottom Line")[
  A single sentence or short bulleted list capturing the most important
  takeaway. This is the "if you read nothing else" version.
]
```

# Background

Brief framing of the project: what problem it was meant to address, who
funded it, the time period covered, and any institutional context that
helps the reader orient. Two or three short paragraphs is plenty.

# Progress on Proposed Work

## Objective 1

Describe the objective as proposed, then what was actually done and what
was learned. Use level-2 headings for each major objective.

## Objective 2

Parallel structure helps readers scan. Include numbers where you have
them — participants, sites, dollars, publications — and cite specific
outputs (meetings held, reports produced, partnerships formed) where you
can.

```{=typst}
#callout(type: "tip", title: "Why it worked")[
  Short reflection on what contributed to success on this objective.
  Optional — use when the lesson is durable beyond this project.
]
```

# Outcomes

Summarize the concrete outputs of the project. A bulleted list is often
the right format here:

- Partnerships established
- Events hosted
- Publications / reports produced
- Data collected
- Institutional commitments secured

# Lessons Learned

Candid reflection on what worked, what didn't, and what you'd do
differently. Funders and future collaborators find this section more
useful than a purely celebratory summary.

```{=typst}
#callout(type: "warning", title: "Caveat")[
  Use the warning style for important caveats, limitations, or points
  where readers might otherwise over-interpret the findings.
]
```

# Next Steps

What comes after this project. If you are seeking continuation funding,
describe the next phase in concrete terms (activities, partners, rough
timeline). If the work is closing, describe how the outputs will be
sustained or handed off.

# Acknowledgments

Brief thanks to funders, collaborators, advisory board members, and
institutional support.
